race.name: Hafjell Ski Marathon
race.tagline: Forty-two kilometers of pure skate through the Øyer highlands — Norway's longest freestyle race and the skating counterpart to the Birkebeiner tradition.

vitals.aid_stations: Feed stations with warm drinks and energy food

climate.description: February in Øyer brings reliable cold and snow. The Hafjell area, known for its alpine resort, sits at moderate altitude with good snow reliability. Typically -12°C to -3°C. Inland Innlandet location provides consistent winter conditions.
challenges: Cold February conditions demand proper layering for skate effort
Exposed sections at altitude can be windy
Skate technique on hilly terrain requires strong fitness
February daylight is limited but improving

course.primary: A 42km skate race through the hills and forests above Øyer in the Gudbrandsdalen valley. The course uses the extensive trail network around Hafjell, a venue known for alpine skiing but with excellent cross-country infrastructure. Rolling terrain with meaningful climbs makes this a demanding skate marathon. The course rewards strong V1 climbing and confident downhill skating.
course.surface: Machine-groomed skate lanes through mountain terrain
course.grooming: High-quality Norwegian grooming on well-maintained trails
features: Hafjell — also an alpine and MTB venue
Rolling terrain rewards strong V1 climbing technique
Part of the Lillehammer-region skiing ecosystem

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Strong snow reliability and accessibility scores — Hafjell is well-connected near Lillehammer. Good grooming and organization for a Norwegian national event. Limited international draw keeps it from higher tiers but strong domestic prestige as Norway's longest skate race.

history.summary: Founded around 1990, the Hafjell Ski Marathon is Norway's longest dedicated skate/freestyle cross-country race at 42km. Based in Øyer, near Lillehammer in the Gudbrandsdalen valley, it uses the trail network around Hafjell — a venue that hosted events during the 1994 Winter Olympics. The race serves as a Birkebeiner seeding event and has become the premier skate marathon in a country dominated by classic-technique races.
notable_facts: Norway's longest dedicated freestyle/skate race at 42km
Hafjell hosted freestyle skiing and biathlon during the 1994 Lillehammer Olympics
Located in the Gudbrandsdalen valley — the historic Birkebeiner heartland
One of the few Norwegian marathons focused exclusively on skate technique
Part of the extensive Lillehammer-region skiing infrastructure
